questions.text: I find that I have to work at reading social situations that others navigate effortlessly.
I find that I can hold someone's pain in mind as a fact to be addressed without it becoming a feeling I experience.
I am the type of person who walks the roadmap alongside others.
I detect unspoken competition between people in professional settings.
I believe that the obligation to respond to suffering is a moral demand that should take precedence over comfort or convenience.
I am highly aware of who initiates conversation and who waits to be approached in social settings.
I believe the best use of my talents is to benefit other people.
I maintain separate spaces or systems for different areas of my life to avoid confusion.
I find that the felt weight of another's suffering is the experience that most consistently reveals my true moral commitments.
I take the lead in negotiations and high-stakes conversations.
I enjoy the weight of responsibility that comes with leading.
I experience fear as a measured alertness rather than a full-body panic.
Loud noises, bright lights, or sudden changes can trigger an outsized emotional response in me.
I allocate specific blocks of time for different activities throughout my day.
I find deep satisfaction in making personal sacrifices for the benefit of my community.
I experience disappointment as a full-body event, not just a passing thought.
I hold the view that it is important to be my own biggest advocate.
I prefer to experiment with a new tool or device hands-on rather than reading instructions.
